title: Split responsibilities
description: The class is doing too many things. Divide responsibilities into cohesive units.

title: Apply Single Responsibility Principle
description: Ensure the class has only one reason to change.

title: Group related methods
description: Identify clusters of related methods and extract them into new classes.

title: Extract domain services
description: Move domain-specific logic into dedicated services.

title: Introduce helper classes
description: Offload utility-style methods into helper classes.

title: Split by lifecycle
description: Separate creation, processing, and persistence responsibilities.

title: Use composition
description: Replace internal logic with composed collaborators.

title: Refactor incrementally
description: Break the class gradually instead of a big rewrite.
example: Extract one responsibility per iteration from {{className}}

title: Introduce strategy pattern
description: Replace large conditional logic with strategy implementations.

title: Modularize internal logic
description: Group logic into internal modules or packages.
example: Move validation logic to validation package

title: Refactor dependency graph first
description: Reduce dependencies before splitting responsibilities.

title: Apply Dependency Inversion
description: Depend on abstractions instead of concrete classes.

title: Introduce Facade
description: Expose a simplified interface while refactoring internals.

title: Break outgoing dependencies
description: Identify and remove unnecessary external dependencies.

title: Split by dependency groups
description: Create new classes based on dependency clusters.

title: Use events instead of direct calls
description: Reduce coupling by using event-based communication.

title: Introduce adapter layer
description: Isolate third-party dependencies behind adapters.

title: Decouple infrastructure code
description: Move infrastructure logic out of the core class.

title: Reduce fan-out first
description: Lower outgoing dependencies before structural refactoring.

title: Refactor under tests
description: Add characterization tests before refactoring.

title: Move behavior into domain model
description: Shift business logic into the entity itself.

title: Encapsulate invariants
description: Ensure domain rules are enforced inside the model.

title: Introduce rich domain methods
description: Replace setters with meaningful behavior.

title: Reduce service-layer logic
description: Move decision-making logic closer to data.

title: Protect internal state
description: Avoid exposing raw fields via getters/setters.

title: Model behavior, not data
description: Think in terms of domain actions.

title: Add domain validation
description: Ensure rules are enforced at entity level.

title: Refactor DTO usage
description: Avoid using entities as pure DTOs.

title: Introduce value objects
description: Encapsulate concepts as value objects.

title: Adopt DDD principles
description: Model domain logic explicitly.
